mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength that lifts corporate earnings forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side constraints that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when INDPRO declines during tightening periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5 for the negative return effect.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.2 while the federal funds rate remains below 4.5%, markets price in aggressive future rate hikes that compress equity valuations and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases when the inflation-rate threshold is breached, invalidating the valuation compression claim.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where housing starts decline while retail sales rise indicates consumers are diverting spending from durable goods to services, signaling economic fragility that triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern produces positive 20-day returns in over 55% of instances, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a disinflationary period confirms supply chain normalization that boosts profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations when INDPRO rises alongside falling CPIAUCSL, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01.
